source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — pre-1928)
title: Sitka, Alaska (1906)
description: 1906 panoramic photograph of Sitka from the parade ground, showing the town spread along the waterfront with snow-capped mountains rising behind it and St. Michael's Cathedral visible at center-right. Taken during the early American territorial period when Sitka had transitioned from Russian capital to American district seat.
alt: 1906 panoramic black-and-white photograph of Sitka, Alaska from the parade ground with snow-capped mountains, cathedral, and waterfront buildings
verifiedBySession: 2026-04-12 — downloaded from tile.loc.gov and visually confirmed
